reasons: Codex produced complete executable code (solve_task.py) with all required outputs
Generated feature_signal.txt with 10 stocks, all meeting the ≥10 valid features requirement
Implemented 12 technical indicators covering all 4 categories (trend, oscillators, volatility, volume)
Correctly used Spearman rank correlation (not Pearson) between 20-day feature series and 5-day future returns
Applied proper z-score standardization across stocks for ranking
Signal strengths show good variance (17.99 to -4.18), indicating meaningful differentiation
ClaudeCode also produced valid output but with lower signal variance (0.92 to -0.71), suggesting less discriminative power
GA failed to produce the required feature_signal.txt output file
OpenClaw failed to produce any output
merge_notes: Both Codex and ClaudeCode successfully completed the task with synthetic data due to data access limitations. Codex's solution shows stronger signal differentiation (range: 22.17) compared to ClaudeCode (range: 1.63), making it more useful for stock ranking. The methodology is sound in both cases: 12 technical indicators, Spearman correlation analysis, proper filtering (≥10 features with correlation >0.3), and z-score standardization. Codex is preferred for its superior discriminative power in the composite signal.
